dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: My father and my brothers, with their flocks and herds and all that they own, have come from the land of Canaan; and behold, they are in the land of Goshen.

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: Your servants are shepherds, both we and our fathers.

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: We have come to sojourn in the land, for there is no pasture for your servants' flocks, because the famine is severe in the land of Canaan. Now please let your servants dwell in the land of Goshen.

dialogue.speaker: pharaoh-of-joseph
dialogue.englishText: Your father and your brothers have come to you.

dialogue.speaker: pharaoh-of-joseph
dialogue.englishText: The land of Egypt is before you; settle your father and brothers in the best of the land, let them dwell in the land of Goshen. And if you know able men among them, put them in charge of my livestock.

dialogue.speaker: pharaoh-of-joseph
dialogue.englishText: How many are the days of the years of your life?

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: The days of the years of my sojourning are one hundred and thirty years; few and difficult have been the days of my life, and they have not attained to the days of my fathers in their sojourning.

dialogue.speaker: egyptians
dialogue.englishText: Give us food! Why should we die in your presence? For our money is gone.

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: Give your livestock, and I will give you food in exchange for your livestock, if the money is gone.

dialogue.speaker: egyptians
dialogue.englishText: We will not hide it from my lord: our money is all spent, and our livestock belong to my lord. Nothing is left before my lord but our bodies and our land.

dialogue.speaker: egyptians
dialogue.englishText: Why should we die before your eyes, both we and our land? Buy us and our land for bread, and we and our land will be servants to Pharaoh. Give us seed so that we may live and not die, and the land not become desolate.

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: Behold, I have today bought you and your land for Pharaoh. Here is seed for you, so sow the land.

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: At the harvests you shall give a fifth to Pharaoh, and four-fifths shall be your own, for seed for the field and for your food, for your households and as food for your little ones.

dialogue.speaker: egyptians
dialogue.englishText: You have saved our lives! Let us find favor in the sight of my lord, and we will be servants to Pharaoh.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: If now I have found favor in your sight, please put your hand under my thigh and deal with me in kindness and faithfulness. Please do not bury me in Egypt.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: But when I lie down with my fathers, you shall carry me out of Egypt and bury me in their burial place.

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: I will do as you have said.
